quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 5 Quiz 4
quiz.subtitle: The Point of Concurrency Formed by the Altitudes
quiz.subject: Geometry

questions.question: An altitude of a triangle is a segment from a vertex that is:
options: Drawn to the midpoint of the opposite side
Drawn to bisect the angle at the vertex
questions.explanation: An altitude is a segment from a vertex of a triangle that is perpendicular to the line containing the opposite side. Every triangle has three altitudes.

questions.question: The orthocenter of a triangle is the point of concurrency of which three lines?
questions.explanation: The orthocenter is the point where the three altitudes of a triangle intersect. Its location depends on the type of triangle.

questions.question: In an acute triangle, the orthocenter lies:
questions.explanation: In an acute triangle, all three altitudes intersect inside the triangle, so the orthocenter is inside the triangle.

questions.question: In a right triangle, the orthocenter is located at:
options: The vertex with the right angle
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, the two legs are altitudes to each other, so they intersect at the vertex containing the right angle. Therefore, the orthocenter is at the right angle vertex.

questions.question: In an obtuse triangle, the orthocenter lies:
options: At the vertex of the obtuse angle
questions.explanation: In an obtuse triangle, the altitudes from the two acute vertices fall outside the triangle, so the orthocenter lies outside the triangle.

questions.question: Which point of concurrency can be located outside the triangle?
options: Both the orthocenter and the circumcenter
questions.explanation: Both the orthocenter and the circumcenter can lie outside the triangle when the triangle is obtuse. The centroid and incenter are always inside the triangle.

questions.question: In $\triangle ABC$, the altitude from $A$ meets $\overline{BC}$ at point $D$. If $\triangle ABC$ is acute, where is point $D$ located?
questions.explanation: In an acute triangle, the foot of each altitude lies on the opposite side between the two vertices, so $D$ is on $\overline{BC}$ between $B$ and $C$.

questions.question: How does the altitude differ from the median?
options: An altitude is perpendicular to the opposite side; a median connects to the midpoint of the opposite side
An altitude connects to the midpoint of the opposite side; a median is perpendicular to the opposite line segment
An altitude bisects an interior corner angle; a median is perpendicular to the base line
An altitude and a median are identical segments across all possible triangular shapes
questions.explanation: An altitude is perpendicular to the opposite side (or its extension). A median connects a vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side. They are different segments except in an isosceles triangle where the altitude from the vertex angle coincides with the median.

questions.question: In an equilateral triangle, the circumcenter, incenter, centroid, and orthocenter are:
options: All located at the same point
Only the centroid and orthocenter coincide
questions.explanation: In an equilateral triangle, all four points of concurrency — circumcenter, incenter, centroid, and orthocenter — coincide at the same point. This is unique to equilateral triangles.

questions.question: The feet of the three altitudes of a triangle are used to form which related triangle?
questions.explanation: The orthic triangle is formed by connecting the feet of the three altitudes. In an acute triangle, these feet lie on the sides; in an obtuse triangle, some lie on side extensions.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the properties of altitudes and the orthocenter, and you can distinguish its location for acute, right, and obtuse triangles.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the basics but review the location of the orthocenter in right triangles (at the right angle) and obtuse triangles (outside).
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: an altitude is perpendicular to the opposite side, the orthocenter is the intersection of altitudes, and its location depends on the triangle type.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! The orthocenter is inside for acute triangles, at the right angle vertex for right triangles, and outside for obtuse triangles. It coincides with the other centers only in equilateral triangles.
